articles.title: After 43 Years, PM Modi Lands In Oslo | Inside The High-Stakes India-Nordic Summit Masterplan
articles.description: History is being scripted in Europe! Prime Minister Narendra Modi has arrived in Oslo for the 3rd India-Nordic Summit, marking the first visit by an Indian PM to Norway since Indira Gandhi in 1983. This is no quiet diplomatic event; it is a vital strategic bridge. As global supply chains face political shocks, India is tapping into five tech powerhouses- Norway, Denmark, Finland, Iceland, and Sweden. We decode how Nordic high-end innovation in AI, semiconductor niches, and green shipping is meeting India’s massive industrial scale and infrastructure pipeline. Watch to see how this compact, leader-driven format is quietly outpacing larger global forums.

articles.title: Delhi HC lists CBI plea in excise policy case for May 25 after bench change
articles.description: The Delhi High Court on Tuesday listed for hearing on May 25 the CBI's petition against the trial court order discharging all accused persons in the liquor policy case and asked the agency to inform former Delhi chief minister Arvind Kejriwal, his deputy Manish Sisodia and former MLA Durgesh Pathak about the change of bench. "Let them be intimated that the case has been allocated to this court. So if they have to say anything or respond to the present petition. Let them be served. Once everybody is here, we will draw a schedule of hearing," Justice Manoj Jain said. The CBI's petition assailing the trial court's decision was listed before Justice Jain after Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma released it from her court last week. On February 27, the trial court discharged Kejriwal, Sisodia and 21 others in the liquor policy case, as it ruled that the case was wholly unable to survive judicial scrutiny and stood discredited in its entirety. Subsequently, Kejriwal, Sisodia, Pathak and some .

articles.title: V D Satheesan Sworn In As Keralam CM | Inside Congress' 20-Member 'Generational Shift' Cabinet
articles.description: A massive political transformation has unfolded in Thiruvananthapuram. Bringing an end to a decade of opposition, the Congress-led UDF has staged a thunderous comeback with V D Satheesan taking oath as the 13th Chief Minister of Kerala. Flanked by top high command leaders including Mallikarjun Kharge, Rahul Gandhi, and Priyanka Gandhi, the mega-event mirrored the party's historic Karnataka show of strength. In this exclusive breakdown, we decode the intense internal power balancing behind the fresh 20-member cabinet from the massive influence of K C Venugopal's loyalists to the crucial 5-berth IUML factor.

articles.title: Breaking: Family demands fair probe in Trisha Sharma death case, raises questions over investigation process
articles.description: The family of Trisha Sharma has intensified its demand for a fair and transparent investigation into her death, alleging serious lapses in the handling of the case and questioning procedural delays in forensic and postmortem examinations. Speaking to the media, family members expressed strong dissatisfaction over what they describe as an incomplete and influenced investigation process, claiming that crucial steps such as detailed postmortem procedures and timely recording of witness statements were either delayed or not properly executed. According to the family, the delay in approvals and procedural formalities has not only complicated the investigation but also raised concerns about the preservation of critical evidence. They argue that the current legal and administrative framework places unnecessary burden on victims’ families, especially when urgent forensic clarity is required. The family has also demanded accountability from investigating authorities, alleging bias and lack of urgency in pursuing key suspects and witnesses linked to the case. The case has further escalated after allegations were made regarding possible influence within institutional systems, including claims of pressure and procedural obstruction. The family insists that individuals connected to the incident, including those present at the scene, should be questioned thoroughly and without delay. They also allege inconsistencies in the initial medical examination and call for a re-evaluation of forensic reports. On the other hand, officials maintain that the investigation is being conducted as per legal protocol and all necessary procedures are being followed under judicial supervision. Authorities have urged the public and media to avoid speculation while the inquiry remains ongoing. The case continues to draw significant attention, with public discourse focusing on the balance between legal procedure and timely justice in sensitive death investigations.

articles.title: Gehlot slams Rajasthan govt over 'severe' fuel shortage, alleges lying
articles.description: Former Rajasthan CM Ashok Gehlot accused the state government of lying about a severe fuel shortage. He stated that petrol pumps in Jodhpur are rationing fuel or have run dry, causing widespread panic and long queues among commuters.

articles.title: Ram Gali in Lahore! Why Pakistan is renaming places to Hindu and Sikh names like Krishan Nagar and Sant Nagar
articles.description: Pakistan Lahore name change: Pakistan’s Punjab government has approved a plan to restore several pre-Partition street and locality names in Lahore, reviving names linked to the city’s Hindu, Sikh, and Jain heritage. The move, backed by former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif, has reignited conversations around Lahore’s lost cultural identity, Partition history, and whether the city is trying to reconnect with its pre-1947 roots.

articles.title: Hantavirus: Britain secures experimental Japanese drug to strengthen response
articles.description: Britain has received experimental favipiravir from Japan to treat a deadly hantavirus outbreak. The drug is not licensed in the UK and its use for hantavirus is experimental. The outbreak involves Andes virus, which can spread between people. Health officials confirm no increased transmissibility or pandemic risk.

articles.title: China rejected Pakistan’s request for sea-based nuclear capability? Leaked files reveal why
articles.description: Pakistan’s reported push for a sea-based nuclear capability, alongside financial and security tensions with Beijing, has exposed emerging strains beneath the two countries’ long-standing “all-weather” partnership, according to leaked documents reviewed by Drop Site News.

Russia begins three days of nuclear drills
articles.description: Russia has commenced a three-day nuclear weapons exercise across its territory. The drills involve over 65,000 soldiers and extensive military equipment. This exercise includes missile launches and participation from aircraft, ships, and submarines. The drills also extend to joint training with Belarus. President Vladimir Putin is scheduled to visit China during this period.

Who was B.C. Khanduri? Former Uttarakhand CM and ex-road transport minister behind India’s massive highway push dies at 91
articles.description: Veteran BJP leader and former Uttarakhand Chief Minister B.C. Khanduri has passed away at 91 after a prolonged illness. A distinguished Major General in the Indian Army, Khanduri later served twice as Uttarakhand CM and as Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways, overseeing the Golden Quadrilateral project. His disciplined approach and commitment to development are widely remembered.

articles.description: Indian Army Chief General Upendra Dwivedi on Tuesday delivered a wide-ranging address at a seminar titled 'Security to Prosperity', organised by the Centre for Land Warfare Studies (CLAWS), drawing direct lessons from Operation Sindoor and outlining a strategic vision for India's role in an increasingly contested global order.
